title: Hierarchy of Cardinality in Sets
description: A visual representation of how sets are categorized by their cardinality, particularly distinguishing between finite, countably infinite, and uncountably infinite sets.
relationships: All sets are categorized by their cardinality (size). A Countably Infinite set has the same cardinality as the natural numbers. An Uncountably Infinite set, like the real numbers, has a strictly larger cardinality than the natural numbers.
